clusters.title: Window rollover can lose or misclassify concurrent URMA samples

member_findings.title: Window rollover can lose or misclassify concurrent URMA samples
member_findings.impact: After this CAS publishes the new window start, another request thread can observe the new window and increment successCount_ or failureCount_ before the rollover thread exchanges those counters to zero on lines 57-58. That new-window sample is then either included in the expired-window snapshot or erased, so concurrent client traffic can produce false worker failovers or miss real URMA data-plane failures.
member_findings.fix: Make rollover linearizable with the counter reset, for example by protecting windowStartTimeMs_ and the counters with one mutex/spin lock, or by using a generation/double-buffer design where recorders increment the counter for the generation they observed and the rollover swaps generations before reading only the sealed old counters.

member_findings.title: Remove committed agent conversation transcript
member_findings.impact: The new .conversations file is not product source, tests, or durable design documentation; it embeds an AI work transcript with partial code snippets and implementation notes that can diverge from the real files. Future searches and reviews will have to distinguish this stale narrative from authoritative source, which adds noise and can mislead maintainers.
member_findings.fix: Drop the .conversations file from the PR. If any rationale from the transcript is useful, move a concise, source-backed explanation into the relevant code comment, design doc, or PR description instead.

clusters.title: Disabled failover still resets atomics on every URMA result

member_findings.title: Disabled failover still resets atomics on every URMA result
member_findings.impact: When `urma_failover_success_rate_ratio` is set to `0.0`, every Set/Get URMA result still calls `RecordUrmaResult` and executes `ResetWindow()`, which performs several atomic stores on the foreground data path. Operators disabling this feature to avoid failover overhead still pay synchronization cost at request frequency.
member_findings.fix: Make the disabled path a cheap no-op after the first transition, for example by tracking an enabled/disabled generation or only clearing the window when the config changes from enabled to disabled.

clusters.title: Add negative tests for the new URMA failover gflag validators

member_findings.title: Add negative tests for the new URMA failover gflag validators
member_findings.impact: The PR adds validation branches for non-finite/out-of-range success ratios and zero min-sample counts, but the added UT/ST coverage only exercises valid values and threshold behavior after the flags are already accepted. A future regression could silently accept an invalid operator config such as ratio > 1, NaN/inf, or min_sample_count=0 without any test failing.
member_findings.fix: Add a focused flags/config test that attempts invalid urma_failover_success_rate_ratio values (<0, >1, NaN/inf if the parser can supply them) and urma_failover_min_sample_count=0, and asserts the validator rejects them; keep the existing valid-value failover tests as-is.

clusters.title: Keep test-only sample counter out of the production API

member_findings.title: Keep test-only sample counter out of the production API
member_findings.impact: GetCurrentSampleCountForTest is declared on the production tracker class solely for tests. Even with the comment, it becomes part of the normal header surface and invites future production call sites to depend on internal counter state that the tracker should own privately.
member_findings.fix: Hide this behind a WITH_TESTS guard, expose it through a test-only friend/helper, or assert the same behavior through settled WindowStats so the production class does not carry test-only API.
